text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously risk-on but a key Fed headline is the real driver - Cleveland Fed's Hammack said AI could fuel inflation and rate hikes may be necessary. That's a direct headwind for high-multiple tech and chip names. Watch rates; if the 10-year yields tick up on this, your sector gets hit first.

Your holdings: PTNM is down -15.25% and close to its $9.50 stop - on margin, this bleeds fast; it needs a decision now. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history - margin amplifies any sudden drop, manage it manually and keep size small. SOXL is a 3x leveraged chip ETF at +4.35% - great when it works, brutal on a reversal; the Fed rate-hike talk today is exactly the catalyst that can crack it.

Watch: 1) How bond yields react to Hammack's comments - rising yields = pressure on your whole book. 2) SOXL's $200 stop; it's your highest-volatility holding.

Bottom line: Protect capital today - the Fed rate-hike warning is the one thing that can hurt every single name you own simultaneously.
plays: PTNM: It's at $10.39, down -15.25%, stop is $9.50 - on margin this is the most urgent risk in your book. Decide now: cut it or honor that stop hard.
SOXL: 3x leverage plus a Fed rate-hike warning is a dangerous combo. Consider trimming to reduce margin exposure; respect the $200 stop with no exceptions.
No new buys today - scanner setups exist but the Fed inflation/rate-hike headline is a real sector headwind; wait for yield reaction to settle before adding risk.
